content.text: Sequencer

The EP-133 features a high-resolution sequencer capable of storing 80,000 notes per project. Record performances in real-time or enter notes step-by-step. Each group can have up to 99 patterns with variable length.
Pads
The 12 pressure-sensitive pads (0-9, +, -) trigger sounds and record notes into the sequencer. Velocity is captured when recording, affecting how loud each note plays back.

content.caption: 12 pads for triggering sounds and recording

content.text: Transport
Control playback with the PLAY and RECORD buttons. Press PLAY to start/stop playback. Press RECORD to arm recording mode - notes played while recording will be captured to the sequencer.

items: SHIFT + PLAY - Restart from beginning without stopping
RECORD while playing - Live record notes to the sequence

content.caption: PLAY and RECORD buttons control transport

content.text: Recording

To record a pattern, press RECORD to arm, then PLAY to start the sequencer. Play your pads in time with the beat - your performance is recorded as the pattern loops. Press RECORD again to stop recording but continue playback.

content.title: Overdub Recording
content.text: With recording enabled, each loop adds to the existing pattern rather than replacing it. This allows you to build up complex patterns layer by layer.
Tempo
Set the project tempo using the TEMPO/LOOP button. Tap TEMPO repeatedly to set tempo by tapping the beat, or hold TEMPO and turn the Y knob to dial in an exact BPM value.
Quantize
Quantization automatically aligns recorded notes to the timing grid. Press TIMING/CORRECT and use +/- to select the quantize resolution: OFF (no quantize), 1/8, 1/16, or 1/32 notes.
Metronome
Toggle the metronome with SHIFT + TEMPO. The metronome helps you record in time by providing an audible click on each beat. The metronome LED indicates when it's enabled.
Swing
Swing adds a shuffle feel by delaying every other note. Adjust swing amount with the Y knob while in tempo mode. Swing is applied per-project and affects all groups.

content.caption: Swing indicator shows current swing amount

content.text: Pattern Length
Patterns can be 1 to 99 bars long. Change pattern length in the pattern settings. Each group can have different pattern lengths, allowing for polyrhythmic arrangements.
Step Sequencing

For precise note placement, you can enter notes step-by-step. Select a step using +/- buttons, then press a pad to place a note on that step. This is useful for programming drums and precise melodies.
Erasing Notes

To erase notes, hold ERASE and press the pad whose notes you want to remove. While the sequencer is playing, this erases notes in real-time as they pass. Hold ERASE and press a group button to clear that entire group's pattern.

content.title: Selective Erasing
content.text: Hold ERASE during playback and press pads at specific moments to erase only those notes - great for cleaning up a busy pattern.
